Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The problem asks us to write a DuckDB SQL query on an event_log table with columns case_id activity and timestamp We need to 1 Identify cases that have the sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order in direct succession no other events in between from the same case 2 Further filter these cases the time difference between Create Order and Confirm Order must be strictly more than 5 days 3 Exclude all events from these specific cases from the final result The result should only contain events from cases that do not meet both the sequence and timing criteria

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets break down step 1 finding cases with the direct succession We can use window functions For each event we can look at the next two activities in the same case Since the events must be in direct succession we can use LEAD activity 1 and LEAD activity 2 partitioned by case_id and ordered by timestamp

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Wait what if two events have the same timestamp The prompt does not specify how to handle ties so well assume timestamp is unique per case or we order by timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait we also need the timestamp of the Create Order and Confirm Order events to check the time difference For an event where activity Approve Purchase Requisition next_activity LEAD activity 1 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp next_next_activity LEAD activity 2 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp create_order_ts LEAD timestamp 1 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp confirm_order_ts LEAD timestamp 2 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp If next_activity Create Order and next_next_activity Confirm Order then the direct succession condition is met

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait what if theres a case where Approve Purchase Requisition occurs multiple times The problem says contains the following sequence of three activities in direct succession If any such sequence exists does it filter the entire case out Yes Identifies any case Among those cases exclude them from your final result

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets carefully read Among those cases that have this specific sequence further refine the selection to only those where the time elapsed between the Create Order event and the Confirm Order event of that sequence is more than 5 days This implies we look at the specific sequence occurrences in the case If a case has the sequence but the time difference is less than or equal to 5 days is it excluded No So a case is only excluded if it has at least one such sequence with a time difference greater than 5 days

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets write a CTE that finds all such sequences

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait is DATEDIFF standard in DuckDB DuckDB supports DATEDIFF day start end It also supports date_diff Wait we can also just subtract timestamps if they are timestamps Since its DuckDB interval arithmetic works next_next_ts minus next_ts greater than INTERVAL 5 DAY Wait if timestamps have fractional days greater than INTERVAL 5 DAY is exactly more than 5 days

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait NOT IN with nulls might be an issue if case_id can be null Usually case_id is not null in process mining but to be safe NOT EXISTS or LEFT JOIN WHERE IS NULL is better Lets use NOT EXISTS

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Another check Does DATEDIFF day create_order_ts confirm_order_ts greater than 5 mean the same thing as confirm_order_ts minus create_order_ts greater than INTERVAL 5 DAY DATEDIFF is imprecise for more than 5 days Using confirm_order_ts minus create_order_ts greater than INTERVAL 5 DAY correctly checks if the exact duration exceeds 5 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: My CTE finds events with activity Approve Purchase Requisition next_activity Create Order and next_next_activity Confirm Order This perfectly checks these three events must appear in this exact order with no other event from the same case in between them because LEAD with ORDER BY timestamp gets the very next event and the one after that

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The query logic is perfectly aligned with the prompt requirements 1 Identifies any case that contains the sequence in direct succession Checked 2 Refine to those where time elapsed between Create Order and Confirm Order is more than 5 days Checked 3 Exclude them from final result Return all events from cases not filtered out Checked
